name: Rayburn Wright
description: American composer, arranger, conductor, trombonist, and music educator (1922-1990)
abstract: Rayburn Wright (27 August 1922 – 21 March 1990) was an American trombonist, composer, arranger and conductor, and professor of jazz studies and contemporary media at the Eastman School of Music, University of Rochester, New York, United States. In 1982 Wright produced the book with accompanying audio cassette, Inside the Score: a detaile…

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Rivello
about_quotes.evidence: when I had finished my master’s at Eastman and I studied with Rayburn Wright and it was fantastic
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Rivello interviewed by Monk Rowe, Rochester, New York, January 11, 2012

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: The transcript only mentions Rayburn Wright in the context of the Interviewer's own lessons; the Interviewee (Dave Liebman) does not mention any personal interaction or relationship with Rayburn Wright.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Maria Schneider
about_quotes.evidence: Rayburn Wright... said, 'we can't accept you... but I really feel like you have talent... why don't you come out to our summer session?'... I worked my butt off to impress Ray and to learn.
about_quotes.doc_title: Maria Schneider interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Ricker
about_quotes.evidence: I was assigned as his graduate assistant and so that turned out to be a very good thing.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ray Ricker interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 27, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Sauter
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...Rayburn Wright left New York... [INTERVIEWEE] And he pulled it off.

about_quotes.speaker: Ellen Rowe
about_quotes.evidence: Dave studied with Rayburn Wright as well so we’re all cut from the same cloth.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ellen Rowe interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 2, 2024
